Event.jsonld-event.read.description: Represents an event ressource withing AstroBook.

Resource-resource.read.description:
Represents a resource within the AstroBook platform.

A resource refers to any reservable asset, such as "Command Module #3",
"Orbital Lab A", or "Hydroponic Chamber 7B". Each resource is linked to a
ResourceType that defines its expected structure and behavior.

id.description: Unique identifier used to track the resource.

type.description: Defines the type this resource belongs to (e.g. "Orbital Lab", "EVA Simulator").

label.description: Short name used to identify the resource in listings and interfaces.

description.description: Optional operational or contextual information about the resource.

ResourceType-resource-type.read.description:
Represents a resource type within the application.

This class defines the structure and attributes that a resource of this type must have.
It allows for dynamic configuration of resource properties based on their type.

Taxonomy-taxonomy.read.description:
Represents a taxonomy within AstroBook.

Taxonomies are used to categorize models in a hierarchical structure,
allowing for organization and classification of content throughout the platform.

User-user.read.description:
Represents a user account within the application.

Used to create, authenticate, and manage users who access the platform,
with permissions defined through configurable roles.
